element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players practice individual events without competition pressure before Olympic mode
Events gradually cycle through: Fencing, Triple Jump, Freestyle Swimming, High Dive, Clay Pigeon Shooting, Hammer Throw, Taekwondo, Pole Vault, Canoeing, Archery, Hurdles, Horizontal Bar
Hang Gliding and Gun Firing (Gun Firing supports NES Zapper optional peripheral) appear as intermission content
Player selects from 28 countries and competes against international opponents; adds narrative framing to event sequences
Two-player head-to-head competition in same sporting events; includes Arm Wrestling (unique to Versus/Exhibition)
Allows players to save progress and resume Olympic mode at previous checkpoint

typical_relief_triggers: Bonus event intermission stages between Olympic rounds
Training mode allows risk-free event practice
Password system breaks allow extended play sessions
typical_tension_triggers: Olympic mode progression with increasing international competition
Event-specific skill requirements (precision shooting, timing-based jumps)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Event-based cycling structure with 12 main competition events per Olympic round, interspersed with 2 bonus stage events; allows natural rhythm breaks between distinct physical activity types

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus events (Hang Gliding, Gun Firing) positioned between Olympic mode rounds; Gun Firing optional control scheme (Controller or NES Zapper peripheral)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Training mode provides low-pressure event familiarization; individual event mastery across varied sport types (fencing, swimming, shooting, gymnastics)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Olympic mode progression requires consistent performance across all 15 events against escalating international competition
notable_spikes: Transition from Training to competitive Olympic mode
Introduction of Versus mode multiplayer competition requiring real-time player response

notable_level_patterns.description: Olympic mode cycles through 12 main sporting events in fixed order, with 2 bonus events positioned between rounds. Each event presents distinct mechanical and skill requirements (input timing, precision, spatial coordination). Events reuse mechanics from prior Track & Field / Hyper Sports titles.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Olympic Mode Round sequence: Fencing → Triple Jump → Freestyle Swimming → High Dive → Clay Pigeon Shooting → Hammer Throw → Taekwondo → Pole Vault → Canoeing → Archery → Hurdles → Horizontal Bar [+ bonus event intermission]

notable_level_patterns.description: Training mode isolates individual events for skill practice; Olympic mode sequences same events within narrative country-selection framework; Versus mode enables competitive event-by-event progression. Same event mechanics, different framing/pressure conditions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Training (safe) → Olympic (moderate competition) → Versus (direct multiplayer confrontation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Gun Firing bonus event supports both standard NES Controller and optional NES Zapper light gun peripheral, allowing players to select input method based on availability/preference.
